nameMeanings.name: Alcorne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from a lost place in East Sussex, Alchhorn, in the parish of Buxted, which was last recorded in 1592. Compare   Allcorn  .

History: This name was taken from Sussex to Scotland and established there in or before the mid 15th century. From Scotland it was taken to Ireland (Donegal) and thence to North America. It was brought to Philadelphia, PA, from northern Ireland in 1721.

variants.name: Allchin
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from a lost place called Alchhorn in Buxted (Sussex), recorded as Alsihorne in 1086, Alchehorne in 1412, and Alchin in 1592. See also Alcorn . The placename is of uncertain origin but contains Old English horn ‘horn’ in some topographic sense.

nameMeanings.name: Jennett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Bennet
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benett
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benet
meanings: From Church Latin benedictus ‘blessed’. This was the name of the saint ( c.480– c.550 ) who composed the Benedictine rule of Christian monastic life that is still followed in essence by all Western orders. He was born near Spoleto in Umbria, central Italy. After studying in Rome, he went to live as a hermit at Subiaco, and later organized groups of followers and imitators into monastic cells. In c.529 he moved to Monte Cassino, where he founded the great monastery that is still the centre of the Benedictine order. His rule is simple, restrained, and practical. See also Bennett .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Jennett Alcorne was born in 1532, in Eastbourne, Sussex, England. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with John Casse. She died in 1590, in Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

ja.lifeSummary: Jennett Alcorneは1532年にイギリススセックスエアストボウルンで生まれた。彼女はJohn Casseとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1590年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロムウエルで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jennett Alcorne(은)는 1532년 영국 수세스 이스트보우른에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Casse(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 크롬웰에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.
